title: Pachypleuria lepida
taxonomy: Pachypleuria lepida is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pachypleuria and is native to tropical Asia. The classification of Pachypleuria lepida places it within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Pteridophyta. As a member of the family Polypodiaceae, it is closely related to other ferns in this family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.
uses: Pachypleuria lepida is known to have various medicinal uses in traditional Asian medicine. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and infections. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other health conditions. The extracts from Pachypleuria lepida are also used in the production of herbal remedies. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of this plant.

intro: Pachypleuria lepida, also known as the hairy polypore or Lepidota, is a species of fungus in the family Meruliaceae, commonly found in tropical forests. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
